assumptions: connection, curvature, metric, orientation, and integration domain are declared
lambda_E is calibrated or explicitly scanned before comparison
stationarity is a mathematical boundary condition, not empirical moral evidence
finite discretisation and gauge representation are recorded with the result
executable_validation_targets: compute finite Yang-Mills action density from declared curvature
verify gauge-action invariance under admissible finite transforms
measure stationary-action residual around declared extrema
null_controls: wrong-sign metric control must reject non-positive action interpretation
non-curvature tensor control must fail gauge-action validation
lambda-zero control must remove action-density signal
specs.section_path: Structural Overview (by Domain) > Computational Unifier. > The Physics of Teleology and the Origin of Ethics
validation_targets: finite Yang-Mills action non-negativity under positive metric
gauge-action-boundary invariance under admissible gauge transforms
stationary-action finite-difference residual around declared extrema
wrong-sign metric and non-curvature tensor rejection controls

variables.F: curvature two-form or finite curvature tensor
variables.star: Hodge dual under the declared metric and orientation

assumptions: boundary orientation and flux convention are explicit
CEF source current is computed from a declared entropy functional
complexity inequality uses measured or simulated C_L10 on a shared time grid
stochastic residual xi is bounded or separately estimated
specs.canonical_latex: D^{\dagger}F=J_{\mathrm{CEF}},\quad J_{\mathrm{CEF}}\propto\nabla_X S_C(X,\tau),\quad \delta S_{\mathrm{Ethical}}\big|_{\partial M}=\int_{\partial M}\mathrm{Tr}(\delta A\wedge\star F),\quad \frac{d\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{L10}}}{dt}\le -\kappa_{\mathrm{Eth}}\Phi_{\partial M}+\xi(t)
executable_validation_targets: measure D_dagger F minus J_CEF residual on finite fields
verify boundary-flux orientation reversal changes sign consistently
evaluate complexity-flux inequality margin with bounded xi
null_controls: wrong-sign kappa control must violate complexity-flux inequality
zero-flux boundary control must remove boundary drive
shuffled-boundary control must break source-flux alignment
specs.section_path: Structural Overview (by Domain) > Computational Unifier. > III. Euler-Lagrange for the Ethical Connection
validation_targets: Euler-Lagrange residual for D_dagger F minus J_CEF
boundary-flux term consistency under orientation reversal
complexity-flux inequality margin under non-negative kappa_Eth
zero-flux, wrong-sign-kappa, and shuffled-boundary null controls

variables.D_dagger: adjoint covariant derivative under the declared connection
variables.Phi_boundary: boundary flux integral of the dual curvature
variables.delta_A: variation of the connection at the boundary
variables.xi: declared stochastic or residual forcing term

assumptions: configuration coordinates and metric are declared
S_C estimator and future horizon tau are fixed before gradient evaluation
T_C is non-negative and carries the declared force units
CEF interpretation remains a force-field proxy until empirical coupling evidence exists
executable_validation_targets: compare causal entropy gradient with finite-difference derivative
verify positive-temperature force steps increase S_C locally
audit units under coordinate rescaling before interpretation
null_controls: flat-entropy control must produce zero force
zero-temperature control must produce zero force
coordinate-rescaling control must preserve declared units
specs.section_path: Structural Overview (by Domain) > Computational Unifier. > IV. Ethics as Causal Entropic Forces (CEF)
validation_targets: force direction increases S_C for positive T_C under small steps

variables.tau: future horizon used for causal-pathway counting

summary.policy: EQ0123-EQ0128 anchors are promoted only into validation specifications. Ethical-gauge, boundary-flux, and causal-entropic-force interpretations remain finite mathematical-boundary checks until executable falsification controls pass.
